families.label: Command
families.help: Bounded, compile-time command cases executed through the public runtime operation.
families.research_question: How do concurrency, workspace scale, command work, and session lifecycle affect command execution?
families.measured_boundary: Explicit-session cells measure command admission and execution; automatic-session cells deliberately include create, publish, and destroy lifecycle.

families.label: File Operations
families.help: Deterministic read, write, edit, and EphemeralOS ownership workloads over published snapshots or live sessions.
families.research_question: How do concurrency, payload, topology, source, and mutation destination affect file operations?
families.measured_boundary: Each request measures exactly one public file operation; fixture setup, verification, and cleanup remain separately timed.

families.label: Workspace Lifecycle
families.help: Concurrent explicit no_op session creation through the exact internal test adapter.
families.research_question: How do workspace scale, network profile, and concurrent session count affect time to ready?
families.measured_boundary: A prepared sandbox and fixture precede the barrier; the measured operation creates C independent sessions and observes readiness.

families.label: LayerStack
families.help: Destructive squash studies over fresh deterministic layer and live-session topologies.
families.research_question: How do storage topology and live-session load affect squash, commit, and remount behavior?
families.measured_boundary: One public manager request includes storage planning, flatten, atomic commit, and the bounded post-commit live-session remount sweep.

profiles.label: Large
profiles.help: Opt-in stress profile with 50,000 files and 2 GiB of deterministic logical content.

profiles.label: Medium
profiles.help: Standard representative profile with 10,000 files and 256 MiB of deterministic logical content.

profiles.label: Small
profiles.help: Standard quick profile with 1,000 files and 16 MiB of deterministic logical content.

operations.label: Execute command
operations.help: Runs one of four compile-time allowlisted bounded shell cases through the public exec_command operation.
operations.measured_boundary: Explicit mode measures command admission and execution against a prepared session; automatic mode deliberately includes create, publish, and destroy lifecycle.
operations.count_semantics_help: Concurrent requests is the number of independent exec_command product requests released in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent requests
factors.help: Independent exec_command requests released together from one trial barrier.

factors.label: Workspace profile
factors.help: Deterministic materialized fixture scale available to the command.

factors.label: Session boundary
factors.help: Explicit measures against a prepared session; automatic includes create, publish, and destroy lifecycle.

factors.label: Command case
factors.help: Compile-time allowlisted bounded shell template; plans cannot submit arbitrary command text.

checks.label: Command exit status
checks.help: The product result reports the case's exact expected exit status.

checks.label: Bounded command output
checks.help: Stored stdout and stderr satisfy the case contract, cap, and digest checks.

checks.label: Command lifecycle
checks.help: Session ownership and cleanup match the selected explicit or automatic boundary.

operations.label: Read file
operations.help: Reads a bounded UTF-8 line window through the public file_read operation.
operations.measured_boundary: One request reads from a verified published snapshot or prepared live session; fixture preparation and content verification remain outside request latency.
operations.count_semantics_help: Concurrent requests is the number of independent file_read product requests released in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent requests
factors.help: Independent product requests released together from one trial barrier.

factors.label: Returned bytes
factors.help: Requested UTF-8 line-window size, bounded by the fixed product output and line caps.

factors.label: Read source
factors.help: Read from the published snapshot or from an explicitly prepared live workspace session.

factors.label: Target mode
factors.help: Independent targets preserve the baseline; same-target mode is an explicit contention treatment.

checks.label: Read window
checks.help: Returned UTF-8 lines exactly cover the requested bounded window.

checks.label: File content hash
checks.help: Observed file content and size match the deterministic fixture expectation.

operations.label: Write file
operations.help: Overwrites deterministic content in a live session or publishes one attributed operation layer.
operations.measured_boundary: One request overwrites an independent or explicitly contended target; session setup or fresh publish topology and later verification are separately timed.
operations.count_semantics_help: Concurrent requests is the number of independent file_write product requests released in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent requests
factors.help: Independent product requests released together from one trial barrier.

factors.label: Content bytes
factors.help: Deterministic non-sparse content bytes written by each request.

factors.label: Mutation destination
factors.help: Session mutates only live workspace state; publish creates one attributed operation layer.

factors.label: Target mode
factors.help: Independent targets preserve the baseline; same-target mode is an explicit contention treatment.

checks.label: File content hash
checks.help: Observed file content and size match the deterministic mutation expectation.

checks.label: Mutation attribution
checks.help: The mutation remains session-local or creates exactly one attributed published layer as selected.

operations.label: Edit file
operations.help: Applies typed ordered exact-string replacements in a live session or one attributed published layer.
operations.measured_boundary: One request applies the configured ordered replacements; deterministic fixture creation, attribution verification, and cleanup remain separately timed.
operations.count_semantics_help: Concurrent requests is the number of independent file_edit product requests released in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent requests
factors.help: Independent product requests released together from one trial barrier.

factors.label: File bytes
factors.help: Deterministic UTF-8 fixture size before ordered exact-string replacements.

factors.label: Replacement count
factors.help: Number of unique ordered exact-string replacements requested and verified.

factors.label: Match density
factors.help: Fraction of deterministic fixture regions containing an exact replacement match.

factors.label: Mutation destination
factors.help: Session mutates only live workspace state; publish creates one attributed operation layer.

factors.label: Target mode
factors.help: Independent targets preserve the baseline; same-target mode is an explicit contention treatment.

checks.label: Replacement count
checks.help: The product applied exactly the requested ordered replacement count.

checks.label: File content hash
checks.help: Observed edited content matches the deterministic expected hash.

checks.label: Mutation attribution
checks.help: The edit remains session-local or creates exactly one attributed published layer as selected.

operations.label: File ownership
operations.help: Reads EphemeralOS per-line ownership from publish auditability through file_blame; this is not Git blame.
operations.measured_boundary: One request resolves ownership for a deterministic published line range; auditability fixture construction and range verification are outside request latency.
operations.count_semantics_help: Concurrent requests is the number of independent file_blame product requests released in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent requests
factors.help: Independent product requests released together from one trial barrier.

factors.label: Line count
factors.help: Deterministic number of published text lines covered by the ownership query.

factors.label: Ownership segments
factors.help: Expected contiguous per-line ownership segments in the published fixture.

factors.label: Auditability events
factors.help: Deterministic publish-auditability events used to construct ownership history.

checks.label: Ownership range coverage
checks.help: Returned ownership ranges are gap-free, ordered, and cover every requested line exactly once.

checks.label: Ownership attribution
checks.help: Every returned range matches the deterministic EphemeralOS publish-auditability owner.

operations.label: Create workspace
operations.help: Benchmarks the exact allowlisted create_workspace_session test adapter for explicit no_op sessions; it is not a public operation.
operations.measured_boundary: C independent session-creation requests start together after one sandbox and fixture are prepared; readiness is measured, while residual cleanup is not.
operations.count_semantics_help: Workspace count C is the number of independent product requests in one measured trial.

factors.label: Concurrent workspace creations
factors.help: Number of independent create_workspace_session requests released from one barrier.

factors.label: Workspace profile
factors.help: Deterministic materialized fixture scale used by every requested session.

factors.label: Network profile
factors.help: Allowlisted network mode applied to each created workspace session.

checks.label: Workspace ready
checks.help: Every successful creation reaches the product's ready state.

checks.label: Network profile
checks.help: The created sessions expose the requested allowlisted network profile.

checks.label: Workspace registry baseline
checks.help: Paired destruction restores the pre-trial workspace-session registry baseline.

operations.label: Squash LayerStack
operations.help: Measures the public manager squash_layerstacks operation, which forwards one internal squash and bounded remount sweep.
operations.measured_boundary: After a fresh deterministic topology and N live sessions are prepared, one squash request measures storage planning, flatten, commit, and the bounded remount sweep.
operations.count_semantics_help: Every measured trial issues exactly one product request; N counts prepared live-session load and never request concurrency.

factors.label: Live sessions N
factors.help: Prepared sessions exposed to the post-commit remount sweep; N is load, never request concurrency.

factors.label: Requested migration ratio
factors.help: Fixture request for the share of live sessions eligible to migrate, rounded to the nearest whole session with halves upward; observed M remains evidence.

factors.label: Remount parallelism W
factors.help: Fixed bounded width of the live-session remount sweep inside the single squash request.

factors.label: Squashable blocks B
factors.help: Number of contiguous deterministic layer blocks prepared for the squash request.

factors.label: Layers per block
factors.help: Published layers in each squashable block of the prepared topology.

factors.label: Payload bytes
factors.help: Deterministically materialized payload bytes contributed by each published layer.

factors.label: Session activity
factors.help: Whether prepared live sessions remain idle or perform bounded deterministic activity before squash.

checks.label: Content equivalence
checks.help: Published content is unchanged across the atomic squash commit.

checks.label: Manifest reduction
checks.help: The committed manifest has the expected reduced layer topology.

checks.label: Session disposition accounting
checks.help: Migrated and non-migrated dispositions account for every requested live session.

checks.label: Session usability
checks.help: Sessions expected to survive the sweep remain usable after remount.

checks.label: LayerStack residue
checks.help: Cleanup leaves no owned topology residue beyond the pre-trial baseline.

phases.label: Total squash
phases.help: Server-observed total duration of the one squash request.

phases.label: Storage plan
phases.help: Server phase that plans the layer-storage rewrite.

phases.label: Flatten
phases.help: Server phase that materializes flattened layer content.

phases.label: Commit
phases.help: Server phase that atomically commits the new manifest.

phases.label: Remount sweep
phases.help: Wall time of the bounded post-commit session sweep.

phases.label: Session remount
phases.help: Per-session remount span observed within the bounded sweep.
